category: Brew recipe dose mistaken for retail bag size

consistency.notes: All non-null/non-empty ground-truth labels are supported by the page text or embedded variant data. The default selected variant is 250g at $22.00 USD, supporting listed_price, bag size, and package_grams. 'is_specialty_coffee' is supported by the page being a roasted single-origin coffee product with detailed origin/producer/process/variety/altitude/harvest/roast information. Boolean negatives are supported: not a blend (single origin Ethiopia / single lot), not espresso (roasted for filter brewing), no indication of decaf, and no indication of co-fermentation/infusion.

realism.reasons: Very plausible as an html-to-text rendering of a real specialty coffee product page. The structure is strongly consistent with Shopify-style coffee roaster pages: nav/header, product title, tasting notes, price, size selector, add-to-cart, installment blurb, details, brew recipe, shipping note, availability, and footer. The voice is natural and on-brand for specialty coffee—concise, sensory, and specific without sounding overengineered. Origin data is coherent: Ethiopia / Gedeb / Gedeo Zone / Yirgacheffe, Danche Washing Station, smallholder contributors, washed process, local varieties like Kurume and Wolisho, and an altitude range that fits the region. The harvest window also reads plausibly for Ethiopia. The descriptive copy about depulping, fermentation, channel washing, and raised-bed drying is standard and believable. Pricing is realistic for a washed Ethiopian at $22/250g and $78/1kg. The variant block is internally consistent with titles, SKUs, and gram weights, and looks like a cleaned-up extraction from product metadata. Minor tells only: the brand itself is generic enough to be invented, and 'This is only our recipe, not the retail bag size' is slightly explanatory in a way that feels tailored to this prompt, but not implausible on a real page.

label.coffee.sensory_text: Jasmine, white peach, Meyer lemon, honeyed black tea. We taste florals up front, a ripe white peach sweetness, Meyer lemon brightness, and a honeyed black tea finish.
label.coffee.display_tasting_notes: Jasmine, white peach, Meyer lemon, honeyed black tea
label.coffee.producer_text: This lot was delivered by growers farming small garden plots around the Danche kebele. Fresh cherry is sorted on arrival, depulped, fermented under clean water, washed through channels, and dried slowly on raised beds.

coffee.sensory_text: This lot was delivered by growers farming small garden plots around the Danche kebele. Fresh cherry is sorted on arrival, depulped, fermented under clean water, washed through channels, and dried slowly on raised beds.
